title: Heresay Homicide — Chapter 2: The Locked Cabinet

answerKey.method: Used the shared passage and locked appraisal cabinet to hide the ledger Elise found before she died
answerKey.motive: To keep a forged consignment ledger from exposing missing dealer payments

answerKey.explanation: Chapter 2: The Locked Cabinet closes one layer of the larger story. This chapter proves the antique store was not background scenery. It was part of the crime path. The proof is rear-room key log, cabinet dust transfer, and ledger page metadata.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Chapter 2: The Locked Cabinet: The Antique Store Connection. Heresay Speakeasy is attached to Circa 1857 antique store, so this murder is solved by tracking what moves through both spaces. This is one continuous story; each chapter gets harder and reveals a deeper layer.
revealScript: Chapter closed. Chapter 2: The Locked Cabinet revealed that Calvin Reed was responsible for this layer. Method: Used the shared passage and locked appraisal cabinet to hide the ledger Elise found before she died. Motive: To keep a forged consignment ledger from exposing missing dealer payments. Key proof: rear-room key log, cabinet dust transfer, and ledger page metadata. Larger-story meaning: This chapter proves the antique store was not background scenery. It was part of the crime path.
